<?php return [ 'feeds' => [ 'main' => [ /* * Here you can specify which class and method will return * the items that should appear in the feed. For example: * [App\Model::class, 'getAllFeedItems'] * * You can also pass an argument to that method. Note that their key must be the name of the parameter: * [App\Model::class, 'getAllFeedItems', 'parameterName' => 'argument'] */ 'items' => '', /* * The feed will be available on this url. */ 'url' => '', 'title' => 'My feed', 'description' => 'The description of the feed.', 'language' => 'en-US', /* * The image to display for the feed. For Atom feeds, this is displayed as * a banner/logo; for RSS and JSON feeds, it's displayed as an icon. * An empty value omits the image attribute from the feed. */ 'image' => '', /* * The format of the feed. Acceptable values are 'rss', 'atom', or 'json'. */ 'format' => 'atom', /* * The view that will render the feed. */ 'view' => 'feed::atom', /* * The mime type to be used in the <link> tag. Set to an empty string to automatically * determine the correct value. */ 'type' => '', /* * The content type for the feed response. Set to an empty string to automatically * determine the correct value. */ 'contentType' => '', ], ], ];
